# Check for new, updated or deleted data
- This script checks for new, updated or deleted data by checking the source data (sap hr) against the target (odw_harmonised_db.employee_dim)
- **New Data:** where a Religion in the source does not exist as an ReligionCode in the target. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Updated data:** Comparison occurs on Religion in source and ReligionCode in targReligionet where the row hash is different i.e. there is a change in one of the columns. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Deleted data:** where an ReligionCode in the target exists but the same Disability_Text doesn't exist in the source. DeletedData flag is set to 'Y'

## View religion_dim_new is created

%%sql
-- Build religion_dim table
-- Gets modified or deleted from source rows

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW religion_dim_new

    AS

-- gets data that matches of SourceID and flags that it is modified based on a row (md5) hash. Flags as "NewData"
-- gets data that is in the target but not in source. Flags as "DeletedData"

SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN T1.ReligionCode IS NULL
        THEN T3.ReligionID
    END as ReligionID, -- surrogate key
    T1.ReligionCode,
    T1.Description,
    T2.SourceSystemID, -- NOT CORRECT
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS IngestionDate,
    NULL AS ValidTo,
    md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.ReligionCode,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Description,'.'))) as RowID,
    'Y' as IsActive,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.ReligionCode = T3.ReligionCode AND md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.ReligionCode,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Description,'.'))) <> T3.RowID
        THEN 'Y'
        WHEN T3.ReligionCode IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END as NewData,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.ReligionCode IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END AS  DeletedData,
    T3.IsActive as HistoricIsActive

FROM religion_grouped T1
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.main_sourcesystem_fact T2 ON "SAP HR" = T2.Description AND T2.IsActive = 'Y'
FULL JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_religion_dim T3 ON T1.ReligionCode = T3.ReligionCode and T3.IsActive = 'Y'
WHERE  
    (
        
        -- flags new data        
        (   CASE
                WHEN T1.ReligionCode = T3.ReligionCode AND md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.ReligionCode,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Description,'.'))) <> T3.RowID
                THEN 'Y'
                WHEN T3.ReligionCode IS NULL
                THEN 'Y'
                ELSE 'N'
            END  = 'Y' ) OR
        
        -- flags deleted data
        (   CASE
                WHEN T1.ReligionCode IS NULL
                THEN 'Y'
                ELSE 'N'
            END = 'Y' )
    )

- This script checks for new, updated or deleted data by checking the source data (protected_characteristics) against the target (odw_harmonised_db.secureInfo_fact)
- **New Data:** where a Ref_No in the source does not exist as an ReligionCode in the target. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Updated data:** Comparison occurs on Ref_No in source and SecureInfoID in target where the row hash is different i.e. there is a change in one of the columns. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Deleted data:** where an SecureInfoID in the target exists but the same Ref_No doesn't exist in the source. DeletedData flag is set to 'Y'

## View secureinfo_fact_new is created

%%sql  

-- Gets modified or deleted from source rows

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW secureinfo_fact_new 

    AS

-- gets data that matches of SourceID and flags that it is modified based on a row (md5) hash. Flags as "NewData"
-- gets data that is in the target but not in source. Flags as "DeletedData"

SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Ref_No IS NULL
        THEN T7.SecureInfoID
        ELSE NULL
    END as SecureInfoID, -- surrogate key
    T1.Ref_No AS SourceID, -- source system id
    T3.DisabilityID,
    T4.DiversityID,
    T5.ReligionID,
    T6.SXOID, 
    T2.SourceSystemID as SourceSystemID,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS IngestionDate,
    NULL AS ValidTo,
    md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Ref_No,'.'), IFNULL(T3.DisabilityID,'.'), IFNULL(T4.DiversityID,'.'), IFNULL(T5.ReligionID,'.'), IFNULL(T6.SXOID,'.'))) as RowID,
    'Y' as IsActive,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Ref_No = T7.SourceID AND md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Ref_No,'.'), IFNULL(T3.DisabilityID,'.'), IFNULL(T4.DiversityID,'.'), IFNULL(T5.ReligionID,'.'), IFNULL(T6.SXOID,'.'))) <> T7.RowID    -- same employee, changed data
        THEN 'Y'
        WHEN T7.SourceID IS NULL -- new employee
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END as NewData,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Ref_No IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END AS  DeletedData, 
    T7.IsActive as HistoricIsActive

FROM odw_standardised_db.vw_protected_characteristics T1
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.main_sourcesystem_fact T2 ON "SAP HR" = T2.Description AND T2.IsActive = 'Y'
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_disability_dim T3 ON    T1.Disability_Text = T3.DisabilityCode  AND 
                                                    T1.Disability_Code_Description = T3.Description AND T3.IsActive = 'Y'
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_diversity_dim T4 ON T1.Ethnic_Origin = T4.Description AND T4.IsActive = 'Y'
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_religion_dim T5 ON  T1.Religion = T5.ReligionCode  AND 
                                                T1.Religious_Denomination_Key = T5.Description AND T5.IsActive = 'Y'
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_sxo_dim T6 ON T1.SxO = T6.Description AND T6.IsActive = 'Y'
FULL JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_secure_info_fact T7 ON T1.Ref_No = T7.SourceID AND T7.IsActive = 'Y'
WHERE
    (
        
        -- flags new data        
        (   CASE
                WHEN T1.Ref_No = T7.SourceID AND md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Ref_No,'.'), IFNULL(T3.DisabilityID,'.'), IFNULL(T4.DiversityID,'.'), IFNULL(T5.ReligionID,'.'), IFNULL(T6.SXOID,'.'))) <> T7.RowID    -- same employee, changed data
                THEN 'Y'
                WHEN T7.SourceID IS NULL -- new employee
                THEN 'Y'
                ELSE 'N'
            END  = 'Y' ) OR
        
        -- flags deleted data
        (   CASE
                WHEN T1.Ref_No IS NULL
                THEN 'Y'
                ELSE 'N'
            END = 'Y' )
    )

# Dataset is created that contains changed data and corresponding target data
- This script combines data that has been updated, Deleted or is new, with corresponding target data
- View **secureinfo_fact_new** is unioned to the target data filter to only those rows where changes have been detected
## View secureinfo_fact_changed_rows is created

%%sql
-- Create new and updated dataset

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY  VIEW secureinfo_fact_changed_rows

    AS

-- gets updated, deleted and new rows 

Select 

    SecureInfoID, -- surrogate key
    SourceID, -- source system id
    DisabilityID,
    DiversityID,
    ReligionID,
    SXOID, 
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
From secureinfo_fact_new WHERE HistoricIsActive = 'Y' or HistoricIsActive IS NULL

    UNION ALL

-- gets original versions of updated rows so we can update EndDate and set IsActive flag to 'N'

SELECT
    SecureInfoID, -- surrogate key
    SourceID, -- source system id
    DisabilityID,
    DiversityID,
    ReligionID,
    SXOID, 
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
FROM odw_harmonised_db.hr_secure_info_fact
WHERE SourceID IN (SELECT SourceID FROM secureinfo_fact_new WHERE SecureInfoID IS NULL) AND IsActive = 'Y';

# View secureinfo_fact_changed_rows is used in a merge (Upsert) statement into the target table
- **WHEN MATCHED** ON the Business Key (i.e. Ref_No from Personal Characteristics), EndDate is set to today -1 day and the IsActive flag is set to 'N'
- **WHEN NOT MATCHED** ON the business key, insert rows
## Table odw_harmonised.secureinfo_fact is updated

%%sql
-- merge into fact table

MERGE INTO odw_harmonised_db.hr_secure_info_fact AS Target
USING secureinfo_fact_changed_rows AS Source

ON Source.SecureInfoID = Target.SecureInfoID

-- For Updates existing rows

WHEN MATCHED
    THEN 
    UPDATE SET
    Target.ValidTo = date_sub(current_timestamp,1),
    Target.IsActive = 'N'

-- Insert completely new rows
WHEN NOT MATCHED 
    THEN INSERT * ;

- This script checks for new, updated or deleted data by checking the source data (sap hr) against the target (odw_harmonised_db.specialism_dim)
- **New Data:** where an EmployeeID in the source does not exist as an EmployeeID in the target. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Updated data:** Comparison occurs on EmployeeID in source and EmployeeID in target where the row hash is different i.e. there is a change in one of the columns. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Deleted data:** where an EmployeeID in the target exists but the same EmployeeID doesn't exist in the source. DeletedData flag is set to 'Y'

## View specialism_dim_new is created

%%sql
-- Build specialism_dim table
-- Gets modified or deleted from source rows

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW specialism_dim_new

    AS

-- gets data that matches of SourceID and flags that it is modified based on a row (md5) hash. Flags as "NewData"
-- gets data that is in the target but not in source. Flags as "DeletedData"

SELECT DISTINCT
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Staff_Number IS NULL
        THEN first(T7.SpecialismID)
        ELSE NULL
    END as SpecialismID, -- surrogate key
    FIRST(T3.EmployeeID) AS EmployeeID,
    T1.Qualification_Name as Qualification,
    T1.Proficien AS Proficiency,
    first(T2.SourceSystemID) as SourceSystemID,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS IngestionDate,
    NULL AS ValidTo,
    md5(concat(IFNULL(FIRST(T3.EmployeeID),'.'), IFNULL(T1.Qualification_Name,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Proficien,'.'))) as RowID,
    first('Y') as IsActive,
    CASE
        WHEN FIRST(T3.EmployeeID) = first(T7.EmployeeID) AND  md5(concat(IFNULL(FIRST(T3.EmployeeID),'.'), IFNULL(T1.Qualification_Name,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Proficien,'.'))) <> first(T7.RowID)    -- same employee, changed data
        THEN 'Y'
        WHEN first(T7.EmployeeID) IS NULL -- new employee
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END as NewData,
    CASE
        WHEN FIRST(T3.EmployeeID) IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END AS  DeletedData, 
    first(T7.IsActive) as HistoricIsActive

FROM odw_standardised_db.vw_specialisms T1
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.main_sourcesystem_fact T2 ON "SAP HR" = T2.Description and T2.IsActive = 'Y'
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_employee_fact T3 ON T1.Staff_Number = T3.EmployeeNumber and T3.IsActive = 'Y'
FULL JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_specialism_dim T7 ON T1.Staff_Number = T7.EmployeeID AND T7.IsActive = 'Y'
WHERE
    (
        
        -- flags new data        
        ( CASE
            WHEN T3.EmployeeID = T7.EmployeeID AND  md5(concat(IFNULL(T3.EmployeeID,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Qualification_Name,'.'), IFNULL(T1.Proficien,'.'))) <> T7.RowID    -- same employee, changed data
            THEN 'Y'
            WHEN T7.EmployeeID IS NULL -- new employee
            THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END  = 'Y' ) OR
        
        -- flags deleted data
        ( CASE
            WHEN T3.EmployeeID IS NULL
            THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END = 'Y' )
    )
    AND
        T1.Staff_Number IS NOT NULL

GROUP BY T1.Staff_Number, T1.Qualification_Name, T1.Proficien
;

select count(*) from specialism_dim_new;
select count(*) from specialism_dim_new where EmployeeID is null;
select * from specialism_dim_new where EmployeeID is null;

## View diversity_dim_new is created

%%sql
-- Build SXO_dim table
-- Gets modified or deleted from source rows

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW sxo_dim_new

    AS

-- gets data that matches of SourceID and flags that it is modified based on a row (md5) hash. Flags as "NewData"
-- gets data that is in the target but not in source. Flags as "DeletedData"

SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Description IS NULL
        THEN T3.SXOID
        ELSE NULL
    END as SXOID, -- surrogate key
    T1.Description,
    T2.SourceSystemID as SourceSystemID, -- NOT CORRECT
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS IngestionDate,
    NULL AS ValidTo,
    md5(T1.Description) as RowID,
    'Y' as IsActive,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Description = T3.Description AND md5(concat(T1.Description)) <> T3.RowID  
        THEN 'Y'
        WHEN T3.Description IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END as NewData,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Description IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END AS  DeletedData,
    T3.IsActive as HistoricIsActive

FROM sxo_grouped T1
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.main_sourcesystem_fact T2 ON "SAP HR" = T2.Description
FULL JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_sxo_dim T3 ON T1.Description = T3.Description and T3.IsActive = 'Y'
WHERE  
    (
        
        -- flags new data        
        ( CASE
            WHEN T1.Description = T3.Description AND md5(concat(T1.Description)) <> T3.RowID
            THEN 'Y'
            WHEN T3.Description IS NULL
            THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END  = 'Y' ) OR
        
        -- flags deleted data
        ( CASE
            WHEN T1.Description IS NULL
            THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END = 'Y' )
    )

- No auto-increment feature is available in delta tables, therefore we need to create new IDs for the inserted rows
- This is done by select the target data and using INSERT OVERWRITE to re-insert the data is a new Row Number
## Table odw_harmonised.SXO_dim is updated

%%sql
-- Insert new SXOID

INSERT OVERWRITE odw_harmonised_db.hr_sxo_dim

SELECT 
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SXOID NULLS LAST) AS SXOID, 
    Description, 
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
FROM odw_harmonised_db.hr_sxo_dim;

# Check for new, updated or deleted data
- This script checks for new, updated or deleted data by checking the source data (sap hr) against the target (odw_harmonised_db.workschedule_dim)
- **New Data:** where a PA in the source does not exist as an Work_Schedule_Rule in the target. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Updated data:** Comparison occurs on PA in source and Work_Schedule_Rule in target where the row hash is different i.e. there is a change in one of the columns. NewData flag is set to 'Y'
- **Deleted data:** where an Work_Schedule_Rule in the target exists but the same PA doesn't exist in the source. DeletedData flag is set to 'Y'

## View workschedule_dim_new is created

%%sql
-- Build workschedule_dim table
-- Gets modified or deleted from source rows

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW workschedule_dim_new

    AS

-- gets data that matches of SourceID and flags that it is modified based on a row (md5) hash. Flags as "NewData"
-- gets data that is in the target but not in source. Flags as "DeletedData"

SELECT DISTINCT
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Work_Schedule_Rule IS NULL
        THEN first(T7.WorkScheduleID)
        ELSE NULL
    END as WorkScheduleID, -- surrogate key
    Work_Schedule_Rule AS WorkScheduleRule, -- source system id
    WkDys AS WorkWeekDays,
    Wk_hrs AS WorkWeekHours,
    Hrs_Day AS WorkDayHours, 
    First(T2.SourceSystemID) as SourceSystemID,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS IngestionDate,
    NULL AS ValidTo,
    md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Work_Schedule_Rule,'.'), IFNULL(T1.WkDys,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Wk_hrs,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Hrs_Day,'.'))) as RowID,
    'Y' as IsActive,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Work_Schedule_Rule = FIRST(T7.WorkScheduleRule) AND
            T1.WkDys = FIRST(T7.WorkWeekDays) AND
            T1.Wk_hrs = FIRST(T7.WorkWeekHours) AND
            T1.Hrs_Day = FIRST(T7.WorkDayHours) AND
            md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Work_Schedule_Rule,'.'), IFNULL(T1.WkDys,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Wk_hrs,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Hrs_Day,'.'))) <> first(T7.RowID)    -- same employee, changed data
        THEN 'Y'
        WHEN first(T7.WorkScheduleRule) IS NULL -- new work schedule
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END as NewData,
    CASE
        WHEN T1.Work_Schedule_Rule IS NULL
        THEN 'Y'
        ELSE 'N'
    END AS  DeletedData, 
    first(T7.IsActive) as HistoricIsActive

FROM odw_standardised_db.vw_absences T1
LEFT JOIN odw_harmonised_db.main_sourcesystem_fact T2 ON "SAP HR" = T2.Description and T2.IsActive = 'Y'
FULL JOIN odw_harmonised_db.hr_work_schedule_dim T7 ON T1.Work_Schedule_Rule = T7.WorkScheduleRule AND
                                                    T1.WkDys = T7.WorkWeekDays AND
                                                    T1.Wk_hrs = T7.WorkWeekHours AND
                                                    T1.Hrs_Day = T7.WorkDayHours AND 
                                                    T7.IsActive = 'Y'
WHERE
    (
        -- flags new data        
        ( CASE
                WHEN T1.Work_Schedule_Rule = T7.WorkScheduleRule AND
                    T1.WkDys = T7.WorkWeekDays AND
                    T1.Wk_hrs = T7.WorkWeekHours AND
                    T1.Hrs_Day = T7.WorkDayHours AND
                    md5(concat(IFNULL(T1.Work_Schedule_Rule,'.'), IFNULL(T1.WkDys,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Wk_hrs,'.'),IFNULL(T1.Hrs_Day,'.'))) <> T7.RowID   -- same employee, changed data
                THEN 'Y'
                WHEN T7.WorkScheduleRule IS NULL -- new work schedule
                THEN 'Y'
                ELSE 'N'
            END  = 'Y' ) OR
        
        -- flags deleted data
        ( CASE
            WHEN T1.Work_Schedule_Rule IS NULL
            THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END = 'Y' )
    )
GROUP BY T1.Work_Schedule_Rule, T1.WkDys, T1.Wk_hrs, T1.Hrs_Day;

# Run tests to check integrity of data by numbers
- This script checks for the total number of current codes in the harmonised table, and compare against the numbers for new data to be added and data to be set as inactive.
- **Changes tracking:** where it checks the data against active records in harmonised and compares with records to add and records to be set as inactive.
- **Changes tolerance levels:** if the total amount to be added and deleted surpasses the tolerance limit, it will interrupt the process of loading the data

# New_Data_Number_Pandas = New_Data_Number.toPandas()

# Dataset is created that contains changed data and corresponding target data
- This script combines data that has been updated, Deleted or is new, with corresponding target data
- View **workschedule_dim_new** is unioned to the target data filter to only those rows where changes have been detected
## View workschedule_dim_changed_rows is created

%%sql
-- Creating new and updated dataset

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW workschedule_dim_changed_rows

    AS

SELECT 
    WorkScheduleID,
    WorkScheduleRule,
    WorkWeekDays,
    WorkWeekHours,
    WorkDayHours,
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
FROM workschedule_dim_new WHERE HistoricIsActive = 'Y' or HistoricIsActive IS NULL

    UNION ALL

SELECT
    WorkScheduleID,
    WorkScheduleRule,
    WorkWeekDays,
    WorkWeekHours,
    WorkDayHours,
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
FROM odw_harmonised_db.hr_work_schedule_dim
WHERE WorkScheduleRule IN (SELECT WorkScheduleRule FROM workschedule_dim_new WHERE WorkScheduleID IS NULL) AND IsActive = 'Y';

# View workschedule_dim_changed_rows is used in a merge (Upsert) statement into the target table
- **WHEN MATCHED** ON the Business Key (i.e. PA from SAPHR), EndDate is set to today -1 day and the IsActive flag is set to 'N'
- **WHEN NOT MATCHED** ON the business key, insert rows
## Table odw_harmonised.workschedule_dim is updated

%%sql
-- merge into fact table

MERGE INTO odw_harmonised_db.hr_work_schedule_dim AS Target
USING workschedule_dim_changed_rows AS Source

ON Source.WorkScheduleID = Target.WorkScheduleID

-- For Updates existing rows

WHEN MATCHED
    THEN 
    UPDATE SET
    Target.ValidTo = date_sub(current_timestamp,1),
    Target.IsActive = 'N'

-- Insert completely new rows
WHEN NOT MATCHED 
    THEN INSERT * ;

# Fix the IDs
- No auto-increment feature is available in delta tables, therefore we need to create new IDs for the inserted rows
- This is done by select the target data and using INSERT OVERWRITE to re-insert the data is a new Row Number
## Table odw_harmonised.workschedule_dim is updated

INSERT OVERWRITE odw_harmonised_db.hr_work_schedule_dim

SELECT 
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY WorkScheduleID NULLS LAST) AS WorkScheduleID,
    WorkScheduleRule,
    WorkWeekDays,
    WorkWeekHours,
    WorkDayHours,
    SourceSystemID,
    IngestionDate,
    ValidTo,
    RowID,
    IsActive
FROM odw_harmonised_db.hr_work_schedule_dim;

# NSIP Project Subscribe

Using a predefined subscription to a topic; read the messages and save them to the data lake

See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/samples/azure/azure-sdk-for-python/servicebus-samples/ for servicebus samples
## Import Logging
%run /utils/py_logging_decorator
## Sets the notebook variables

from notebookutils import mssparkutils

#destination variables
jobId = mssparkutils.env.getJobId()
storage_account=mssparkutils.notebook.run('/utils/py_utils_get_storage_account')
raw_storage_container = 'abfss://odw-raw@' + storage_account
raw_storage_source_folder = 'odt/'
raw_storage_folder_path = raw_storage_container + raw_storage_source_folder

#service bus variables
secret_name="odt-message-queue-connection-string"
kv_linked_service="ls_kv"
topic_name="nsip-project"
subscription_name="nsip-project-odw"
max_records = 10

import json
from datetime import date
import os


def writeData(logger, filename, data):
    try:
        raw_storage_sub_folder = topic_name +'/' +str(date.today())
        destination_dir = '/synfs/' +jobId +'/temp_raw/' +raw_storage_sub_folder+'/'
        #create the destination_directory, if it doesn't already exist
        os.makedirs(destination_dir, exist_ok=True)

        with open(f"{destination_dir}{filename}", "w") as f:
            json.dump(data, f)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception(e)
        return False
    return True
## Get the latest messages from the subscription and write to the data lake

@logging_to_appins
def saveFromSubscription():
    from azure.servicebus import ServiceBusClient, ServiceBusMessage
    import json
    import logging
    logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
    akv_name=spark.sparkContext.environment.get('keyVaultName', 'get')
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils
    conn_str = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(akv_name, secret_name, kv_linked_service)

    

    ### mount the data lake storage in Synapse to the Synapse File Mount API
    try:
        #connect to the service bus
        logger.info("Connecting to Service Bus")
        servicebus_client = ServiceBusClient.from_connection_string(conn_str=conn_str, logging_enable=True)
        logger.info("Mounting " +raw_storage_folder_path)
        mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw")
        mssparkutils.fs.mount(raw_storage_folder_path, "/temp_raw", {"linkedService":"ls_storage"}) 
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception(e)
        return False

    try:
        with servicebus_client:
            receiver = servicebus_client.get_subscription_receiver(
                topic_name=topic_name,
                subscription_name=subscription_name
            )

            with receiver:
                
                while True:
                    received_msgs = receiver.receive_messages(max_message_count=max_records, max_wait_time=5)
                    
                    if len(received_msgs) > 0:
                        logger.info("Reading " +str(len(received_msgs)) +" messages")

                        for message in received_msgs:
                            #first json.loads unescapes it, second parses it into a json object
                            data = json.loads(str(message))
                            filename = str(message.message_id) +'.json'

                            #save data to blob
                            if writeData(logger, filename, data):
                                receiver.complete_message(message)
                                logger.info("Wrote message to file" +filename)
                            else:
                                logger.error("Failed to write message " +filename)
                    else:
                        logger.info("No messages to process")
                        break
        return True        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception(e)
        return False
    finally:
        #cleanup
        logger.info("Unmounting " +raw_storage_folder_path)
        mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw")
        logger.info("Finished")
saveFromSubscription()

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW nsip_relevant_reps_new 

    AS

SELECT

T1.CaseReference,

T1.ContactID,
T1.OrganisationName,
T1.JobTitle,
T1.FullName,
T1.PhoneNumber,
T1.EmailAddress,
T1.BuildingNumber,

T1.Town,
T1.County,
T1.Country,
T1.Postcode,
T1.NSIPRelevantRepresentationID as RelevantRepID,
T1.AttendPrelimMeeting,
T1.DateReceived,
T1.Notes,
T1.AgentContactId,
T1.RepresentAtCompAcqHearing,
T1.RepresentAtIssueHearing,
T1.RepresentAtOpenFloorHearing,
T1.RepresentationOriginal,
T1.RepresentationRedacted,
T1.SubmitLaterReps,
T1.WebReference,
T1.RelevantRepStatus as RelevantRepresentationStatus,
T1.OwnerOrOccupier,

FROM odw_harmonised_db.casework_nsip_relevant_representation_dim T1
WHERE T1.IsActive = 'Y'

# View nsip_relevant_reps_new is used in a merge (Upsert) statement into the target table
- **WHEN MATCHED** ON the Business Key (i.e. RelevantRepID), update rows
- **WHEN NOT MATCHED** ON the business key, insert rows
## Table odw_curated.nsip_relevant_reps is updated

%%sql

-- merge into nsip_relevant_reps table

MERGE INTO odw_curated_db.nsip_relevant_reps AS Target
USING nsip_relevant_reps_new AS Source

ON Source.RelevantRepID = Target.RelevantRepID

WHEN MATCHED AND Source.RowID<>Target.RowID
    THEN
    UPDATE SET *
WHEN NOT MATCHED
    THEN
    INSERT *

@logging_to_appins
def azfileshare_new_files(akv_name:str, secret_name:str, kv_linked_service:str, share_name:str, directory_path:str, jsonschema:str, target_container:str, target_folder:str, db_name:str, table_name:str):
    '''
    Function which determines if any new files are available in the datalabs environment
    
    Args:
        akv_name (str): Name of the key vault resource
        secret_name (str): Name of the secret
        kv_linked_service (str): Name of the key vault linked service in Azure Synapse
        share_name (str): Azure File Share resource name
        directory_path (str):  Root path to establish ShareDirectoryClient connection to 
        jsonschema (str): Pyspark StructType representation of a dataframe schema 
        target_container (str): Container containing the Fileshare logs
        target_folder (str): Folder containing the Fileshare delta table
        db_name (str) : name of the database the table will be created within
        table_name (str) : name of the table the parquet will be associated with

    Returns:
        Nothing

    Actions:
        Appends any new files to the fileshare logs (a delta table) from a Fileshare
        
    Raises:
        ValueError("Empty directory has been provided") - avoids a Fileshare client directory which is empty

    '''
    ### start datetime REMOVE?
    import datetime
    dt = datetime.datetime.now()


    ### establish connection to azure files and create client
    from azure.storage.fileshare import ShareDirectoryClient
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils
    creds = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(akv_name, secret_name, kv_linked_service)
    parent_dir = ShareDirectoryClient.from_connection_string(conn_str=creds, share_name=share_name, directory_path=directory_path)

    ### recursive function to find all files within a directory
    all_files = []
    def _recursive_find_all_files(share_directory_client):
        files_and_dirs = list(share_directory_client.list_directories_and_files())
        if files_and_dirs == []:
            raise ValueError("S2R-SAPHR: Empty directory has been provided")
        ### add some ms type checks 
        # import azure.storage.fileshare
        # isinstance(my_list[0], azure.storage.fileshare._models.DirectoryProperties)
        files_and_dirs_len = len(files_and_dirs)
        count = 0
        for li in files_and_dirs:
            if li['is_directory']:
                _recursive_find_all_files(share_directory_client.get_subdirectory_client(li['name']))
            else:
                fileprops = share_directory_client.get_file_client(li['name']).get_file_properties()
                all_files.append([dt, fileprops.path, fileprops.last_modified]) # correct this to timestamp?
                count += 1
                if files_and_dirs_len == count:
                    break

    _recursive_find_all_files(parent_dir)
    
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    from pyspark.sql.functions import lit
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

    from pyspark.sql.types import StructType
    import json

    schema = StructType.fromJson(json.loads(jsonschema))
    logs_now_df = spark.createDataFrame(all_files, schema)
    logs_table_df = spark.read.format("delta").load(target_container+target_folder)
    filtered_cols = logs_now_df.columns
    filtered_cols.remove("Process_Date")
    logs_now_df_compare = logs_now_df.select(filtered_cols)
    logs_table_df_compare = logs_table_df.select(filtered_cols)
    new_paths = logs_now_df_compare.subtract(logs_table_df_compare)
    new_paths = new_paths.withColumn("Process_Date", lit(dt))

    # new_paths.write.format("delta").mode("append").saveAsTable(f"{db_name}.{table_name}")
    if new_paths.count() == 0:
        mssparkutils.notebook.exit("[]")
    else:
        all_paths = [paths["Filepath"] for paths in new_paths.collect()]
        mssparkutils.notebook.exit(",,,".join(all_paths))

@logging_to_appins
def create_table_from_schema(jsonschema:str, db_name:str, table_name:str, target_container:str, target_folder:str, change_data_feed=False):
    '''
    Function to create a table from a schema definition. Schema merge is disabled.
    
    Args:
    jsonschema (str) : json representation of the dataframe schema
    db_name (str) : name of the database the table will be created within
    table_name (str) : name of the table the parquet will be associated with
    target_container (str) : storage container within which the parquet will be located
    target_folder (str) : folder within which the filelog and parquet file will be located
    
    Returns:
        Nothing

    Actions:
        Creates a table if the table doesn't already exist in a target location 
        
    Exceptions:
        NameError(f"{db_name} doesn't exist") - generated if the desired database doesn't exist already
        ValueError("The table is represented by multiple parquet files- please investigate!")
        ValueError(f"Table already exists but the location provided doesn't match the existing parquet location for the table!, {table_deets}, {target_loc}")
    '''
    ### type checks

    ### imports
    from pyspark.sql.types import StructType
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    import json
    from delta import DeltaTable
    import logging
    logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

    ### define target location
    target_loc = target_container + target_folder
    
    ### use json to create dataframe
    schema = StructType.fromJson(json.loads(jsonschema))
    df = spark.createDataFrame([], schema)

    ### table checks
    if any([dbs.name == db_name for dbs in spark.catalog.listDatabases()]):
        if any([table.name == table_name for table in spark.catalog.listTables(db_name)]):
            table_details = spark.sql(f"DESCRIBE DETAIL {db_name}.{table_name}").toPandas()
            if len(table_details) > 1:
                raise ValueError("S2R-SAPHR: The table is represented by multiple parquet files- please investigate!")
            else:
                if table_details['location'][0].rstrip("/") == target_loc.rstrip("/"):
                    logger.info(f"S2R-SAPHR: Table already exists with correct location")
                else:
                    table_deets = table_details['location'][0]
        
                    raise ValueError(f"S2R-SAPHR: Table already exists but the location provided doesn't match the existing parquet location for the table!, {table_deets}, {target_loc}")
        else:
            ### create spark sql table from parquet
            if any([dbs.name == db_name for dbs in spark.catalog.listDatabases()]):
            ### write df to parquet location
                if not (DeltaTable.isDeltaTable(spark,target_loc)):
                    df.write.option("mergeSchema", "false").format('delta').save(target_loc)# no overwrite on table creation
                    
                if not any([table.name == table_name for table in spark.catalog.listTables(db_name)]):
                    spark.sql("CREATE TABLE {0}.{1} USING DELTA LOCATION '{2}'".format(db_name,table_name,target_loc))
                    if change_data_feed == True:
                        spark.sql("ALTER TABLE {0}.{1} SET TBLPROPERTIES (delta.enableChangeDataFeed = true)".format(db_name,table_name))
    else:
        raise NameError(f"{db_name} doesn't exist")

@logging_to_appins
def ingest_raw_to_standardised():
    '''
    Function reads all the schedules for all files, and processes any new files into standardised, and adds outstanding files into an outstanding files table
    Parameters:
    None

    Returns:
    None

    Actions:
    Processes files from raw to standardised based on a schedule, and will also keep track of files that haven't arrived and attempt to process those

    '''
    ### module imports
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    import logging
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
    import json
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils
    import pandas as pd
    logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    from datetime import datetime, timedelta, date
    from pyspark.sql.types import StringType,BooleanType,DateType,TimestampType,IntegerType, FloatType, StructType
    from delta.tables import DeltaTable
    from croniter import croniter
    import re
    from pyspark.sql.functions import col, lit
    import calendar

    ### use the ADLS gen2 assoicated with the synapse resource in any environment (DR/Dev/Stage/Prod)
    storage_acc_name = spark.sparkContext.environment.get('dataLakeAccountName', 'get')

    ### create outstanding files table
    outstanding_files_table_json = spark.read.text("abfss://odw-config@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/orchestration/scheduling_outstanding_files_table.json", wholetext=True).first().value
    create_table_from_schema(outstanding_files_table_json, "odw_config_db", "raw_to_std_outstanding_files", "abfss://odw-standardised@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/", "config/raw_to_std_outstanding_files" )

    ### loads json for scheduling
    orchestration_json = spark.read.text("abfss://odw-config@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/orchestration/orchestration.json", wholetext=True).first().value
    json_config_dict = json.loads(orchestration_json)
    
    ### process outstanding files
    outstanding_df = spark.read.format("delta").load("abfss://odw-standardised@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/config/raw_to_std_outstanding_files")
    ### make sure there are entries in the outstanding files table
    if outstanding_df.count() > 0: 
        outstanding_df_pd = outstanding_df.toPandas()
        for pdidx, pdrow in outstanding_df_pd.iterrows():
            time_now = pdrow['Expected_Datetime'] 
            outstanding_source_id = pdrow['DefinitionID'] 
            definitions = json_config_dict['definitions']
            for defs in definitions:
                ### isolate the scheduling config for the entry in the oustanding files table
                if defs['Source_ID'] == outstanding_source_id: 
                    standardised_container = "abfss://odw-standardised@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/" # defs['Standardised_Container'] + "/"
                    standardised_table_name = defs['Standardised_Table_Name']
                    standardised_path = defs['Standardised_Path'] + "/"
                    standardised_table_loc = "abfss://odw-config@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/" + defs['Standardised_Table_Definition']
                    standardised_table_def_json = spark.read.text(standardised_table_loc, wholetext=True).first().value
                    create_table_from_schema(standardised_table_def_json, "odw_standardised_db", standardised_table_name,standardised_container , standardised_path+standardised_table_name)
                    deltadays = defs['Expected_Within_Weekdays']

                    ### expected from - change from normal process
                    last_start_dt = time_now 
                    
                    ### expected to - change from normal process
                    next_end_dt = last_start_dt + timedelta(days=deltadays) 

                    if last_start_dt <= time_now < next_end_dt: 
                        standardised_table_df = spark.read.format("delta").load(standardised_container+standardised_path+standardised_table_name)
                        ### check if the file has already been ingested
                        rows = standardised_table_df.filter((standardised_table_df.expected_from == last_start_dt) &  (standardised_table_df.expected_to == next_end_dt)).count()
                        
                        if rows > 0 :
                            logger.info("File has already been ingested")
                        else:
                            source_container_path = "abfss://odw-raw@"+storage_acc_name+".dfs.core.windows.net/" 
                            if defs["Source_Frequency_Folder"] == "":
                                source_folder =  defs['Source_Folder'] +"/"
                            ### handle weekly / monthly sub folders
                            else:                            
                                source_folder =  defs['Source_Folder'] +"/"+ defs["Source_Frequency_Folder"] + "/"
                            ### find correct weekly folder 
                            date_folders = mssparkutils.fs.ls(source_container_path+source_folder)
                            for datex in date_folders:
                                date_string = datex.name 
                                ### handle YYYY-MM-DD dates
                                if len(date_string) == 10:
                                    folder_date = datetime.strptime(date_string, "%Y-%m-%d")
                                ### handle YYYY-MM dates
                                elif len(date_string) == 7:
                                    folder_date = datetime.strptime(date_string, "%Y-%m")
                                    
                                    folder_date = date(folder_date.year, folder_date.month, calendar.monthrange(folder_date.year, folder_date.month)[-1]) + timedelta(days=1)
                                    folder_date = datetime.combine(folder_date, datetime.min.time())
                                else:
                                    raise ValueError("Date format in raw folder is incorrect, should be YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM")
                                if last_start_dt <= folder_date < next_end_dt:
                                    source_folder_with_date = mssparkutils.fs.ls(source_container_path+source_folder+date_string)
                                    if source_folder_with_date == []:
                                        error_folder_string = source_container_path+source_folder+date_string
                                        raise RuntimeError(f"Folder is empty within {error_folder_string}")
                                    for file in source_folder_with_date:
                                        filename = file.name 
                                        ### isolate the correct file
                                        if defs['Source_Filename_Start'] in filename:
                                            jobId = mssparkutils.env.getJobId()
                                            ### mount the data lake storage in Synapse to the Synapse File Mount API
                                            mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw") 
                                            mssparkutils.fs.mount( 
                                            source_container_path+source_folder+date_string, 
                                            "/temp_raw", 
                                            {"linkedService":"ls_storage"} 
                                            )   
                                            ### open .csv and .xlsx files using pandas
                                            if ".xlsx" in filename.lower():
                                                sheet_name = defs['Source_Sheet_Name'] if 'Source_Sheet_Name' in defs else 0
                                                df = pd.read_excel(f"/synfs/{jobId}/temp_raw/{filename}", dtype=str, sheet_name=sheet_name)
                                            elif '.csv' in filename.lower():
                                                df = pd.read_csv(f"/synfs/{jobId}/temp_raw/{filename}", quotechar='"',escapechar='\\',encoding="utf8",dtype=str)
                                            else:
                                                raise RuntimeError(f"This file type for {filename} is unsupported")
                                            ### drop headerless columns
                                            df = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.str.contains('^Unnamed')]
                                            rows_raw = len(df.index)
                                            mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw") 
                                            ### convert to pandas dataframe
                                            sparkDF=spark.createDataFrame(df)
                                            ### add date columns included with every standardised table
                                            sparkDF = sparkDF.withColumn("ingested_datetime",lit(datetime.now()))
                                            sparkDF = sparkDF.withColumn("expected_from",lit(last_start_dt))
                                            sparkDF = sparkDF.withColumn("expected_to",lit(next_end_dt))
                                            schema = StructType.fromJson(json.loads(standardised_table_def_json))
                                            
                                            ### remove characters that Delta can't allow in headers and add numbers to repeated column headers
                                            cols_orig = sparkDF.schema.names
                                            cols=[re.sub('[^0-9a-zA-Z]+', '_', i).lower() for i in cols_orig]
                                            cols=[colm.rstrip('_') for colm in cols]
                                            newlist = []
                                            for i, v in enumerate(cols):
                                                totalcount = cols.count(v)
                                                count = cols[:i].count(v)
                                                newlist.append(v + str(count + 1) if totalcount > 1 else v)
                                            for colix in range(len(cols_orig)):
                                                sparkDF = sparkDF.toDF(*newlist) 

                                            ### mappings for Json schema definitions to Pyspark data types
                                            mapping = {
                                                'string': StringType(),
                                                'timestamp': TimestampType(),
                                                'integer': IntegerType(),
                                                'date': DateType(),
                                                'float': FloatType()
                                            }

                                            ### column data type casting
                                            for field in json.loads(standardised_table_def_json)['fields']:
                                                sparkDF = sparkDF.withColumn(field['name'], col(field['name']).cast(mapping[field['type']]))
                                            colheaders = []
                                            for field in json.loads(standardised_table_def_json)['fields']:
                                                colheaders.append(field['name'])
                                            sparkDF = sparkDF.select(colheaders)
                                            sparkDF2 = spark.createDataFrame(sparkDF.rdd, schema=schema)
                                            ### writing the dataframe to the existing standardised table
                                            sparkDF2.write.format("delta").mode("append").saveAsTable(f"odw_standardised_db.{standardised_table_name}")

                                            standardised_table_df_new = spark.read.format("delta").load(standardised_container+standardised_path+standardised_table_name)
                                            rows_new = standardised_table_df.filter((standardised_table_df.expected_from == last_start_dt) &  (standardised_table_df.expected_to == next_end_dt)).count()

                                            ### test correct number of rows have written
                                            if rows_raw == rows_new:
                                                mssparkutils.fs.mkdirs(source_container_path+"archive"+"/"+source_folder+date_string+"/")
                                                mssparkutils.fs.cp(source_container_path+source_folder+date_string+"/"+filename,source_container_path+"archive"+"/"+source_folder+date_string+"/"+filename)
                                                spark.sql(f"DELETE FROM odw_config_db.raw_to_std_outstanding_files WHERE Expected_Datetime = '{time_now}' and DefinitionID = {outstanding_source_id}")
                                                logger.info('All rows have successfully written')
                                            ### delete transaction if incorrect number of rows has written and raise an error
                                            else:
                                                deltaTable_standardised = DeltaTable.forPath(spark, standardised_container+standardised_path+standardised_table_name)
                                                fullHistoryDF = deltaTable_standardised.history()
                                                fullHistoryDF.select("version").first()['version']
                                                deltaTable_standardised.restoreToVersion(fullHistoryDF.select("version").first()['version'] - 1)
                                                raise RuntimeError(f"Incorrect number of rows have been written to the standardised table {standardised_table_name}, rolled back to original version")
ingest_raw_to_standardised()

@logging_to_appins
def validate_parameters(storage_acc, raw_container, raw_source_folder, raw_name, standardised_container, standardised_source_folder, standardised_name, linked_service, sheet_name=0, header_row=0):
    
    ''' 
    Type check the parameters that will be used in the ETL processing with pyspark in subsequent functions    

    Args:

    Returns:

    Actions:
    
    Raises:
     '''
    
    ### imports
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils
    import logging
    
    ### creating filepath
    filepath = raw_container + raw_source_folder + raw_name

    ### checking abfss path
    if filepath.split('abfss://')[0] != '':
        raise ValueError("File path string is not of abfss:// type")
        
    
    ### checking filepath has xlsx file in it 

    ### checking str data types
    # defining parameters to check
    parameters = {
        'filepath':filepath,
        'storage_acc':storage_acc,
        'linked_service':linked_service
    }

    # checking str type
    str_errors = ''
    for para in parameters:
        if type(parameters[para]) != str:
            str_errors += para
        if str_errors != '':
            raise TypeError(f"Parameters: {str_errors} not of type string")   
            
    # checking sheet name type is str or int
    if type(sheet_name) != str and type(sheet_name) != int:
        raise TypeError("Sheet name should either be a string or a integer")
        
    # checking int type
    if type(header_row) != int:
        raise TypeError("Header row should be an integer value")

def _cleanse_headers(df):
    '''
    NOT A PURE FUNCTION
    Take a dataframe and appropriately cleanse headers in a file
    '''
    ### imports
    import pandas as pd
    import re

    ### type checks
    if not isinstance(df, pd.DataFrame):
        raise TypeError("Expecting a pandas dataframe")
        
    cols = df.columns
    cols=[re.sub("( |\#|\# |\.|\(|\)|\/)","_",i) for i in cols] # replace chars with _
    cols=[re.sub("([\_]{2,})","_",i) for i in cols] #replace consecutive _ with one _
    cols=[re.sub("(\_)$","",i) for i in cols] #remove trailing _
    cols = [item.replace(',','') for item in cols]
    df.columns = cols

    return df 

def _change_types(df):
    '''
    NOT A PURE FUNCTION
    Take a dataframe and appropriately make all data types a string
    '''

    ### imports
    import pandas as pd
    
    ### type checks
    if not isinstance(df, pd.DataFrame):
        raise TypeError("Expecting a pandas dataframe")
        
    return df.astype(str, errors='ignore')

@logging_to_appins
def ingest_excel_to_parquet(storage_acc, raw_container, raw_source_folder, raw_name, standardised_container, standardised_source_folder, standardised_name, linked_service, sheet_name=0, header=0):

    '''
    Create a parquet file with delta capabilities from a source excel file
    '''

    ### initialising spark session
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

    ### setting appopriate spark variables
    source_full_storage_account_name = storage_acc
    spark.conf.set(f"spark.storage.synapse.{source_full_storage_account_name}.linkedServiceName", linked_service )
    spark.conf.set(f"fs.azure.account.oauth.provider.type.{source_full_storage_account_name}", "com.microsoft.azure.synapse.tokenlibrary.LinkedServiceBasedTokenProvider")

    ### creating targetpath variable
    filepath = raw_container + raw_source_folder + raw_name
    targetpath = standardised_container + standardised_source_folder + standardised_name

    ### reading file with abfss file location format
    import pandas as pd
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils
    jobId = mssparkutils.env.getJobId()
    mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw") 
    mssparkutils.fs.mount( 
    raw_container + raw_source_folder, 
    "/temp_raw", 
    {"linkedService":"ls_storage"} 
    )   
    df = pd.read_excel(f"/synfs/{jobId}/temp_raw/{raw_name}", sheet_name=sheet_name, header=header, dtype='object')
    mssparkutils.fs.unmount("/temp_raw") 
    ### cleanse headers 
    df = _cleanse_headers(df)
    
    ### change all types to string
    df = _change_types(df)

    ### writing output
    df.to_parquet(targetpath)

@logging_to_appins
def enable_delta_on_parquet(standardised_container, standardised_source_folder):
    '''
    Take a standard parquet file and enable delta lake capabiltiies on the file
    '''

    ### initialise spark session
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
    spark.conf.set('spark.hadoop.orc.overwrite.output.file','true')

    ### create target delta folder variable
    target_delta_folder = standardised_container + standardised_source_folder

    ### check parquet file in targetpath exists
    ### OUTSTANDING

    ### converting parquet to delta table
    from delta.tables import DeltaTable
    if not DeltaTable.isDeltaTable(spark,target_delta_folder):
        deltaTable = DeltaTable.convertToDelta(spark, f"parquet.`{target_delta_folder}`")

@logging_to_appins
def lakedb_table_sparksql(db_name,delta_lake_table_name,standardised_container, standardised_source_folder):
    '''
    Make delta parquet file available in lake database
    '''

    ### imports and spark session creation
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
    from delta.tables import DeltaTable
    spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

    ### create target delta folder variable
    target_delta_folder = standardised_container + standardised_source_folder

    ### check parquet file is now delta table
    ### outstanding

    ### make delta table available to be queried in spark sql
    spark.sql(f"drop table if exists {db_name}.{delta_lake_table_name};")
    spark.sql(f"CREATE TABLE {db_name}.{delta_lake_table_name} USING DELTA LOCATION '{target_delta_folder}'")
